title: Per-source affiliate parameter injection
description: A small per-source affiliate resolver, analogous to the existing Source contract (sources/base.py), that transforms a destination URL by injecting affiliate/referral parameters when a source has an affiliate programme configured. Driven entirely by server-side config.
acceptance_criteria: Each marketplace source can declare an optional affiliate resolver (config-driven, no code change needed for a simple query-param scheme)
Sources without an affiliate programme configured pass the URL through unchanged
Resolver logic is unit-testable independent of the Flask route (pure function: source + url + config -> destination url)
No affiliate partner ID, tag, or secret ever appears in a template, client-side script, or API response
Adding/removing a source's affiliate programme requires only a config change, not a schema migration or code change to existing listing rows
notes: See docs/adr/0002-affiliate-link-redirect-and-tracking.md. Deliberately mirrors sources/base.py's per-source contract style rather than inventing a new pattern. Shipped 2026-07-08 as outbound.py: MarketplaceAdapter (ABC) + PassthroughAdapter/QueryParamAffiliateAdapter, dispatched by MarketplaceOutboundService. Pure-function unit tests in tests/test_outbound.py (19 tests, no Flask/DB). See docs/implementation-notes/2026-07-08-2135-marketplace-outbound-gateway-epic-101.md.
